title: Chunkset Manifest
description: Phase 7b Subtask 12: per-chunkset sidecar manifest emitted alongside the chunks.jsonl file at LibV2/courses/<slug>/dart_chunks/manifest.json (Phase 7b) and LibV2/courses/<slug>/imscc_chunks/manifest.json (Phase 7c). The schema is symmetric across both chunkset kinds (decision #6 from the Phase 7b-prep investigation): a single `chunkset_kind` discriminator plus a conditional source-SHA requirement keeps the dart and imscc sidecars on a single contract. Validated by lib/validators/chunkset_manifest.py::ChunksetManifestValidator (Phase 7b ST 13). Forward-references the Triangle-invariant chain (decision #8) — full chain validation across PDF -> DART HTML -> chunks lands as a Phase 7c followup against the `source_*_sha256` fields below.

chunks_sha256.description: SHA-256 (hex, lowercase) of the sibling chunks.jsonl file. The validator (ST 13) recomputes the on-disk SHA and fail-closes on mismatch so a tampered or partially-written chunks.jsonl is detected at archive time.

chunker_version.description: Version of the chunker-schema contract that produced the chunks. MIGRATION DRIFT (post-Phase-8 review): this field's semantics shifted from 'Python-package release version of ed4all-chunker' (resolved via importlib.metadata.version('ed4all-chunker'), e.g. '0.1.0') to 'chunker-schema-contract version' (resolved via Trainforge.chunker.CHUNKER_SCHEMA_VERSION, e.g. 'v4') after the ed4all-chunker workspace package was folded back into Trainforge/chunker/. The contract version is bumped only when the emit shape or semantics change, decoupled from any Python-package release cadence. Pattern alternation accepts BOTH shapes: the new ^v\d+$ schema-version form ('v4') AND the legacy ^\d+\.\d+\.\d+(?:[+-]...)?$ semver form ('0.1.0', '0.1.0+missing', '1.0.0-rc1') so any pre-migration manifest on disk — including the '0.0.0+missing' fallback sentinel emitted before Phase 7's editable-install landed — continues to validate. Mirrors the chunker_version pattern in course_manifest.schema.json.

chunkset_kind.description: Which chunkset this manifest describes. 'dart' is the Phase 7b chunkset emitted from staged DART HTML during the `chunking` workflow phase (sibling files at `LibV2/courses/<slug>/dart_chunks/`). 'imscc' is the Phase 7c chunkset emitted from a packaged IMSCC at `LibV2/courses/<slug>/imscc_chunks/` (renamed from the legacy `corpus/` directory per ST 15). The discriminator drives the conditional source-SHA requirement below.

source_dart_html_sha256.description: SHA-256 (hex, lowercase) of the staged DART HTML inputs that produced this chunkset. For multi-file inputs, the SHA is computed over the deterministic concatenation of input files (filename-sorted then content-concatenated) so the value is stable across re-runs. Required when chunkset_kind == 'dart'; absent when chunkset_kind == 'imscc'. Forward-link to Triangle-invariant chain validation (decision #8): the source_pdf_sha256 → source_dart_html_sha256 → chunks_sha256 chain is validated end-to-end as a Phase 7c followup.

source_imscc_sha256.description: SHA-256 (hex, lowercase) of the packaged IMSCC zip that produced this chunkset. Required when chunkset_kind == 'imscc'; absent when chunkset_kind == 'dart'. Forward-link to Triangle-invariant chain validation (decision #8): pairs with the upstream packaging phase output to anchor the chunks → IMSCC provenance edge.

chunks_count.description: Number of chunks (lines) in the sibling chunks.jsonl file. Optional but recommended — the validator (ST 13) cross-checks this against the actual line count when present.

generated_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (UTC) recording when the chunkset was emitted. Optional. Mirrors the import_timestamp / export_timestamp shape used elsewhere in the LibV2 manifest family.
